claude-code.note: Native Claude Code Task tool — no key needed

codex.note: Codex CLI — uses your ChatGPT login, no key needed

kimi-cli.note: Kimi CLI — uses your Kimi login, no key needed

claude-cli.note: Claude Code CLI — uses your Claude login, no key needed

aristotle.note: Harmonic Aristotle formal theorem prover — requires ARISTOTLE_API_KEY

opus._note: Claude Opus 4.6. Primary route: Claude Code Task tool (subscription, no per-token cost). MUST use Task tool — claude-cli spawns a nested CLI which fails inside Claude Code. Extended thinking is inherent. OpenRouter fallback available.

chatgpt-5.4._note: ChatGPT 5.4. Latest flagship coding model, 1M context window. Primary route: Codex CLI (subscription, reasoning_effort=xhigh). Codex CLI defaults cap at ~272K — codex_config overrides enable full 1M. OpenRouter fallback.

gpt-5.3-codex._note: GPT-5.3 Codex. Previous flagship. Superseded by chatgpt-5.4. Kept for historical model-history.json compatibility.

gpt-5.2._note: GPT-5.2. Primary route: Codex CLI (subscription, reasoning_effort=xhigh). OpenRouter fallback with reasoning.effort=xhigh.

gemini-3.1-pro._note: Gemini 3 Pro. Primary route: Google API (thinkingLevel=HIGH). OpenRouter fallback with reasoning.effort=xhigh.
Gemini 3.1 Pro. 2.5x reasoning improvement over 3.0. Primary: Google API.

gemini-3-flash._note: Gemini 3 Flash. Primary route: Google API (thinkingLevel=HIGH + grounding). OpenRouter fallback without grounding.

kimi-2.5._note: Kimi 2.5. Primary route: Kimi CLI (subscription, --thinking flag). Built-in <think> reasoning. OpenRouter/Moonshot API fallback.

glm-5._note: GLM-5. OpenRouter only (no CLI available). Built-in <think> reasoning. $0.75/$2.55 per 1M.

minimax-m2.5._note: MiniMax M2.5. OpenRouter only (no CLI available). Built-in <think> reasoning. $0.20/$1.00 per 1M.

aristotle._note: Formal theorem prover — not a solver, used as verification layer in Step 3.5

cli_primary._description: CLI-first: Claude/GPT/Kimi via native CLIs (subscription — no per-token cost), Gemini via Google API, GLM-5/MiniMax via OpenRouter. 10 models, max reasoning on all.

multi_model._description: Maximum diversity — each solver is a different provider, debaters standardized at 2xOpus + 2xGPT

multi_model_full._description: Different providers for solvers, standardized 2xOpus + 2xGPT debaters

balanced._description: All Claude via native CLI/Task tool

max_quality._description: Best from each provider — 2xOpus + 2xGPT debaters for standardized intelligence

grade_loop._description: Grade improvement loop: full model pool with Gemini 3.1 Pro for iterative report grading

budget._description: Zero Claude subagents — all calls via Codex/Kimi CLI or OpenRouter API. Only the main Opus orchestrator runs Claude. Saves Claude subagent costs while maintaining multi-model diversity.

formal_verification._description: Aristotle formal verification layer (Step 3.5). Runs IN PARALLEL with debaters — adds zero latency to critical path.
formal_verification._note_speed: Aristotle is SLOW (1-15 min per proof). That's why Step 3.5 runs as background tasks alongside debaters. max_claims=1 keeps it to ~5 proofs max. Each proof gets 10s×12=2min before timeout. Claims still running at RWEA time are marked INCONCLUSIVE.
formal_verification._note_disable: Set enabled=false to skip formal verification entirely. skip_domains can list domains where formalization adds little value (e.g. ['strategy']).
